install.note: Python-based agent by Nous Research. Requires Linux or macOS (WSL2 on Windows). After install, run 'source ~/.bashrc' then 'hermes --version' to verify.

configure.note: For LOCAL installations, EchoBird's App Manager handles model configuration automatically via ~/.echobird/hermes.json. This section is for REMOTE server deployments via Mother Agent (SSH).

first_run.description: Hermes uses 'hermes config set' to configure models. This routes secrets to ~/.hermes/.env and settings to ~/.hermes/config.yaml automatically. Supports any OpenAI-compatible endpoint via OPENAI_BASE_URL + OPENAI_API_KEY.

required_commands.hermes config set OPENAI_API_KEY: (string) API key for the LLM provider

common_mistakes: WRONG: manually writing to ~/.hermes/.env with echo/cat (may cause duplicate entries)
RIGHT: using 'hermes config set KEY VALUE' (handles routing automatically)
WRONG: using --provider custom (not a valid provider name)
RIGHT: just set OPENAI_BASE_URL + OPENAI_API_KEY + model, hermes auto-detects

field_mapping.description: EchoBird sends model data in the '[Attached models for deployment]' block. Map to Hermes config as follows:
field_mapping.attachment_baseUrl: -> hermes config set OPENAI_BASE_URL <value> (append /v1 if needed)

steps: 1. Parse the '[Attached models for deployment]' block to extract: name, model (=modelId), baseUrl, apiKey.
2. Ensure baseUrl ends with /v1. If not, append /v1.
6. Verify: hermes chat -q 'Hello, respond with OK' --quiet

cli.description: Hermes uses 'hermes chat -q' for non-interactive oneshot mode.
cli.role_mechanism: Hermes uses SOUL.md at ~/.hermes/SOUL.md for agent identity. EchoBird writes role content here; Hermes auto-detects changes on next session.
cli.output_format: With --quiet flag, outputs clean text response only.
cli.note: Hermes is Python-based, by Nous Research. Supports 200+ models via OpenRouter, plus MiniMax, Kimi, OpenAI, Anthropic, custom endpoints. No native Windows support (WSL2 required).
